title: cirsium eriophorum var. oviforme
intro: Cirsium eriophorum var. oviforme, commonly known as the woolly thistle, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family that is native to the mountainous regions of western North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of woolly thistle include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it a potential treatment for various conditions such as eczema, acne, and infections. Woolly thistle can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is often used in herbal remedies to promote skin health and immune function.
